item.title: Conditional Compilation in C++
item.description: Conditional compilation allows to conditional define what instructions can be compiled and what to skip.

item.title: define directive in C++
item.description: Macros are used to define identifiers which can then be replaced the text they represent.

item.title: `#include` directive
item.description: When processor encounters #include directive, it replaces directive with the content of the included file.

item.title: Preprocessors in C++
item.description: Preprocessor are one step before the actual compilation. It does works like removing the comments ensuring there are new lines at the file end.

item.title: Namespaces in C++
item.description: A namespace is a scope region which contains names. A namespace identifies where the particular name (a function, types etc).

item.title: Writing multiple code files
item.description: When we have code divided into multiple files, we need to forward declare the function in use and provide the file that contains the function definition while compiling the program.

item.title: Variable Initialization and Assignment
item.description: Initialization is a processing assigning an initial value to a variable while declaring it.

item.title: Objects and Variables in C++
item.description: An object represents a region in the storage (RAM or CPU registers) that can be used to store values.

item.title: Warning Configurations in Compiler
item.description: We can configure the compiler to show warning diagnostics for the programs so that we can make the program better.

item.title: Compiler Extensions
item.description: Compiler extensions are compiler specific implementations.
